summary: Russia launched a large-scale missile and drone barrage on the night of 30 May, targeting multiple regions and claiming 284 enemy assets destroyed. Ukrainian forces reported several UAV incursions from the Black Sea and over northern oblasts, while a Russian drone struck a residential building in Galati, Romania. Intelligence indicates heightened threat of Iskander‑M ballistic launches from Bryansk and ongoing SBU strikes on Russian command sites.
key_points: Russia executed a coordinated attack with an Iskander‑M ballistic missile, six H‑101 cruise missiles and 290 Shahed‑type drones, reporting 284 enemy targets neutralised (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
A Russian drone entered Romanian airspace and collided with a multi‑storey building in Galaţi during an attack on Odesa, causing a fire and casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81538).
Ukrainian air defence intercepted 5 H‑101 missiles and 279 Shahed‑type drones across northern, southern, central and eastern Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
SBU used drones to disable a Russian FSB radio‑electronic intelligence node in the Krasnodar region, disrupting missile and UAV targeting (Source: @UkraineNow/81537).
Ukrainian monitors warned of imminent Iskander‑M launches from Bryansk oblast, raising high alert for ballistic threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67272).
Two rockets were reported over Kremenchuk, and additional rockets struck Cherkasy, Vinnytsia and other central locations (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67265).
UAVs launched from the Black Sea were observed heading toward Odesa, while others approached the Zaporizhzhia‑Dnipropetrovsk border area (Source: @kpszsu/63276; @kpszsu/63282).

sections.executive_summary: Russia executed a coordinated attack with an Iskander‑M ballistic missile, six H‑101 cruise missiles and 290 Shahed‑type drones, reporting 284 enemy assets neutralised (Source: @kpszsu/63300). Ukrainian air defence intercepted the majority of these weapons across multiple fronts (Source: @kpszsu/63300). Simultaneously, a Russian drone struck a residential building in Galaţi, Romania, highlighting the conflict’s cross‑border escalation (Source: @UkraineNow/81538).

points: Ukrainian air defence units shot down five H‑101 cruise missiles and 279 Shahed‑type drones, covering northern, southern, central and eastern sectors, demonstrating extensive layered defence (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
The SBU employed drones to strike a Russian FSB radio‑electronic intelligence facility in the Krasnodar region, temporarily disabling Russian targeting capabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81537).

Ukrainian monitoring channels reported two rockets over Kremenchuk and additional rockets over Cherkasy and Vinnytsia, indicating ongoing Russian missile pressure on central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67265).
Ukrainian authorities warned of a high threat level for Iskander‑M launches from Bryansk, prompting heightened alertness in affected regions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67272).

Russian forces launched an Iskander‑M ballistic missile from Bryansk oblast, accompanied by six H‑101 cruise missiles from Vologda and a swarm of 290 Shahed‑type drones targeting Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
A Russian combat drone penetrated Romanian airspace and collided with a residential building in Galaţi, causing fire and injuries, marking a cross‑border escalation (Source: @UkraineNow/81538).
Russian UAVs were observed taking off from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, suggesting maritime launch points for aerial attacks (Source: @kpszsu/63276).
Additional Russian UAV activity was reported near the Zaporizhzhia‑Dnipropetrovsk border, indicating pressure on Ukraine’s southern industrial zones (Source: @kpszsu/63282).
UAVs were tracked moving from the south‑east toward Kryvyi Rih, indicating Russian aerial pressure on the Dnipropetrovsk industrial corridor (Source: @kpszsu/63302).
Russian missile and drone barrage included launches from Kursk and Orel regions, striking eastern oblasts such as Kharkiv and Donetsk (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
Ukrainian air defence reported successful interception of multiple Shahed drones over eastern sectors, limiting damage to infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
SBU drone strike on a Russian FSB site in Krasnodar may affect command‑and‑control links supporting eastern operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81537).
UAVs were observed over northern Chernihiv heading toward Snovsk and Semenivka, indicating Russian reconnaissance activity (Source: @kpszsu/63325).
Monitored UAV flights from the Black Sea towards Odesa could also be routed over northern coastal areas, expanding the threat envelope (Source: @kpszsu/63276).
Air defence intercepted Shahed drones over northern Ukraine as part of the 284‑target tally (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
Russian missile launches from Bryansk and Kursk regions targeted northern oblasts, prompting alerts in Kyiv and surrounding districts (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
UAVs launched from the Black Sea were directed toward Odesa, posing a direct threat to southern port facilities (Source: @kpszsu/63276).
Russian missile and drone attacks included strikes on Zaporizhzhia, with UAVs reported near the border of Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @kpszsu/63282).
Air defence reported downing of Shahed drones over southern Ukraine as part of the large‑scale 30 May operation (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
Ukrainian rail infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia was hit, resulting in casualties and damage to electric and heat trains (Source: @UkraineNow/81554).

On the night of 30 May, Russia deployed an Iskander‑M ballistic missile, six H‑101 cruise missiles and 290 Shahed‑type drones, with Ukrainian forces claiming 284 enemy assets destroyed (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
Ukrainian air defence successfully intercepted 5 H‑101 missiles and 279 Shahed drones across multiple fronts, demonstrating robust anti‑air capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
A Russian drone entered Romanian airspace and struck a residential building in Galaţi, highlighting the conflict’s expanding aerial reach (Source: @UkraineNow/81538).
UAV activity from the Black Sea toward Odesa and from the north toward Chernihiv indicates diversified launch vectors used by Russian forces (Source: @kpszsu/63276; @kpszsu/63325).

key_developments: Russia launched an Iskander‑M ballistic missile, six H‑101 cruise missiles and 290 Shahed‑type drones, reporting 284 enemy targets neutralised (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
Ukrainian air defence shot down five H‑101 missiles and 279 Shahed drones across northern, southern, central and eastern Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
A Russian combat drone entered Romanian airspace and collided with a multi‑storey building in Galaţi, causing fire and casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81538).
SBU used drones to disable a Russian FSB radio‑electronic intelligence site in the Krasnodar region, disrupting missile and UAV targeting (Source: @UkraineNow/81537).
Ukrainian monitors warned of imminent Iskander‑M launches from Bryansk oblast, raising high alert for ballistic threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67272).
UAVs launched from the Black Sea were observed heading toward Odesa, indicating maritime launch points for Russian aerial attacks (Source: @kpszsu/63276).
Two rockets were reported over Kremenchuk, with additional rockets striking Cherkasy and Vinnytsia, showing continued Russian missile pressure on central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67265).
sections.threat_assessment: The coordinated missile‑drone barrage demonstrates Russia’s capability to conduct large‑scale, multi‑vector strikes, stressing Ukrainian air defence across all fronts. Cross‑border drone incursions into Romania reveal an expanding operational reach that could threaten neighboring NATO states. Continued UAV launches from the Black Sea and northern corridors increase pressure on critical infrastructure, while the threat of Iskander‑M launches from Bryansk underscores a persistent ballistic missile danger.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine’s air defence reported high interception rates, indicating effective but strained defensive layers. NATO allies, notably Romania, have been urged to coordinate air‑space monitoring and defence measures. Ukrainian intelligence agencies are intensifying surveillance of launch sites in Bryansk and the Black Sea to pre‑empt further attacks.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring channels provide real‑time alerts on missile trajectories and UAV movements, essential for rapid threat dissemination. The SBU’s drone strike on the Russian FSB node suggests a growing Ukrainian capability to target enemy C2 assets. Continued reporting of rocket impacts in central oblasts highlights the need for reinforced civil defence in those regions.
